title: Are the manga entertainment D R EoE DVDs fullscreen
display_title: Are the manga entertainment D R EoE DVDs fullscreen
wikitext:
[]: Are the manga entertainment D/R/EoE DVDs fullscreen?             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Wed Nov 12, 2008 8:42 pm Is watching Death for the fullscreen worthwhile? I mean, does the ep. 24 fight look more fantastic, or is it just the TV version recropped? Is EoE's widescreen side very nice with this release?

[]: Re: Are the manga entertainment D/R/EoE DVDs fullscreen?             Quote Post by chazthesilencer » Thu Nov 13, 2008 2:12 pm Nothing looks more fantastic in Death, you'd do yourself a favour by saving your money and not buying it.

[]:             Quote Post by Rubedo » Thu Nov 13, 2008 2:38 pm truth! Only reason i bought D&R was because it came bundled with EoE and it worked out cheaper (£8 for D&R + EoE or £25 for EoE alone.....)

[]:             Quote Post by majlund » Thu Nov 13, 2008 3:45 pm The Eva franchise is strong.

[]:             Quote Post by Count A'ight » Sun Nov 16, 2008 4:33 am Widescreen = fail.

[]:             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Sun Nov 16, 2008 6:03 pm Wow, reminds me of how if someone played BioShock in widescreen, they were actually playing the game with the top and bottom of the screen slashed off.

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Sun Nov 16, 2008 6:05 pm Sounds like Death is an apt name for such a product.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Nov 16, 2008 7:09 pm Hopefully you all aren't including the handful of cuts that were specifically redone for DEATH in this generalized assessment of the movie.

[]:             Quote Post by Count A'ight » Sun Nov 16, 2008 9:30 pm Iron Rooster wrote: Wow, reminds me of how if someone played BioShock in widescreen, they were actually playing the game with the top and bottom of the screen slashed off.

[]:             Quote Post by SeanTucker » Thu Nov 27, 2008 4:10 pm

[]:             Quote Post by Space Penis » Thu Nov 27, 2008 6:40 pm It might not be pretty, but I still think Death is probably the best installment of Eva, Rebuild notwithstanding (Not seen any of that yet). I think it's probably because Eva has always, at least for me, always been more focussed on the characters than the plot, and Death is primarily structured as a character study rather than just a recap of the series. Y'know what, Talking about character studies, I'm going to use this as an opportunity to pimp this old thing I did with Death ages ago.
